title: GitHub Connector Onboarding Plan

onboarding_phases.name: GitHub App Creation
onboarding_phases.objective: Create a dedicated GitHub App for USBAY GitHub connector actions after governance approval, with no repository mutation enabled until later validation phases complete.
completion_criteria: GitHub App metadata captured without private key material.
Initial permission scope is recorded and approved.
No live action capability is enabled until later phases pass.

onboarding_phases.name: Credential Authority
onboarding_phases.objective: Establish accountable authority over GitHub App credentials, installation scope, rotation, revocation, and incident response.
completion_criteria: Credential owner and revocation owner are explicitly recorded.
Repository and capability scope are bounded.
Authority record is audit-linked and exportable.

onboarding_phases.name: Secret Storage
onboarding_phases.objective: Store GitHub App private key material outside the repository in an approved secrets system with access control, rotation evidence, and revocation evidence.
completion_criteria: No private key or token is stored in repository evidence.
Secret reference is hash-only.
Access, rotation, and revocation authorities are recorded.

onboarding_phases.name: Permission Mapping
onboarding_phases.objective: Map every enabled GitHub capability to a minimum permission, approval requirement, policy reference, and audit requirement.
completion_criteria: Only approved capabilities are present.
Every mutation maps to required approval and audit output.
Permission scope is no broader than the selected pilot capability.

onboarding_phases.name: Approval Workflow
onboarding_phases.objective: Require policy reference, approval reference, actor attribution, audit receipt, and rollback path before any production GitHub mutation.
completion_criteria: Approval gate blocks unapproved mutation attempts.
Every action is attributable to a GitHub App actor and a USBAY human approval reference.
Rollback path is documented before the action is allowed.

onboarding_phases.name: Pilot Repository Validation
onboarding_phases.objective: Validate one repository-scoped, non-destructive pilot capability before broader production onboarding.
completion_criteria: Credential validity is proven without exposing secrets.
Permission validity is proven against the pilot capability.
Audit and receipt evidence are generated.
Fail-closed behavior is proven for missing approval, bad permission, missing audit, and connector failure.

onboarding_phases.name: Production Readiness Review
onboarding_phases.objective: Decide whether the GitHub connector can move from pilot validation to production-governed onboarding.
completion_criteria: All prior phases are complete.
No critical fail-closed gaps remain.
Human governance review produces an explicit go/no-go decision.

github_app_requirements.installation_scope: Install only on repositories explicitly approved for USBAY governance.
github_app_requirements.repository_scope: Start with one pilot repository; broader repository access requires separate approval and evidence.

permission_scope.default: minimum required for one pilot capability
permission_scope.expansion_rule: Any new permission requires updated permission mapping, human approval, and audit evidence.

rotation_requirements.private_key_rotation: at least every 90 days or immediately after incident indicators

secret_governance.storage_authority: Approved secret manager or protected deployment secret store with audit access; repository storage is forbidden.
secret_governance.access_authority: Only connector runtime identity and designated secret custodians may access the secret reference.
secret_governance.rotation_authority: USBAY governance authority and credential owner must approve scheduled or emergency rotation.
secret_governance.revocation_authority: USBAY governance authority may revoke credentials immediately on compromise, scope mismatch, or policy violation.
incident_response_path: verify no active token remains trusted

actor_attribution_model.usbays_human_actor: human approver reference bound to the action before execution
actor_attribution_model.audit_rule: GitHub App actor alone is insufficient; USBAY human approval reference must be present for governed mutations.

rollback_path_requirements: rollback blocked if evidence is incomplete

credential_validity: Token issuance evidence exists without logging token material.
permission_validity: Pilot capability maps to a minimum permission.
No unused write permission is enabled.
Repository allow-list contains only pilot repository.
audit_generation: Pre-action audit record generated before connector call.
Post-action receipt captured after connector response.
approval_enforcement: Valid approval proceeds only after pre-action audit succeeds.

governance_controls_before_first_live_mu: GitHub App exists with repository-scoped installation.
Credential is stored outside the repository.
Credential authority, access authority, rotation authority, and revocation authority are documented.
Permission mapping is approved for the exact pilot capability.
Post-action GitHub receipt capture is required.
Fail-closed tests pass for missing credential, missing approval, permission mismatch, audit failure, receipt failure, and connector failure.

acceptance_answers.can_github_app_onboarding_be_executed_sa: Yes, onboarding can be executed safely only as a phased governance-controlled process. Live mutation remains blocked until credential authority, secret storage, permission mapping, approval enforcement, audit generation, receipt capture, rollback, and pilot validation evidence exist.

readiness_decision.reason: The governance-controlled onboarding sequence is fully defined and can be followed without violating fail-closed governance. This does not authorize production activation or live mutation; it only authorizes moving into governed onboarding when the required evidence is collected phase by phase.

generated_pr_metadata.commit_title: PB-043 VERIFIED: GitHub Connector Onboarding Plan
generated_pr_metadata.pr_title: PB-043 VERIFIED: GitHub Connector Onboarding Plan

generated_pr_body:
## PURPOSE
PB-043 defines the governance-controlled onboarding sequence for the first production GitHub connector using the GitHub App model selected by PB-042.

## RISK
GitHub onboarding can create mutation authority over branches, pull requests, releases, rulesets, protections, and secrets. If onboarding skips credential authority, secret governance, approval enforcement, audit generation, or fail-closed validation, USBAY could create an ungoverned execution path.

## POLICY LINK
AGENTS.md fail-closed, audit-first engineering, human oversight, branch governance, network governance, and secret/data hygiene rules. PB-038 connector framework, PB-040 connector readiness, PB-041 GitHub connector authority, and PB-042 GitHub production connector design.

## REQUIRED APPROVALS
USBAY-AUDIT and USBAY-GLOBAL23 review are required before merge. Future GitHub App creation, credential storage, live API calls, or repository mutation require separate approval and evidence.

## GOVERNANCE CHECKS
JSON evidence must parse. Summary must answer onboarding safety, exact sequence, controls before first live mutation, required evidence, and final decision. Diff hygiene, conflict marker scan, metadata validation, and required governance section checks must pass.

## AUDIT
PB-043 generates governance/evidence/pb043/github_connector_onboarding_plan.json and governance/evidence/pb043/github_connector_onboarding_summary.md. It defines phase evidence, audit outputs, fail-closed conditions, and completion criteria without creating credentials or performing external actions.

## IMPACT
USBAY gains a controlled path from design to GitHub App onboarding while keeping all live mutations blocked until every phase produces required evidence.

## Decision
VERIFIED

## Status
READY_FOR_REVIEW
